title: Evolving treatment strategies in anal squamous cell carcinoma: Integrating chemoradiotherapy, immunotherapy, and emerging biomarkers.
abstract: Anal squamous cell carcinoma (SCAC) is a relatively rare but increasingly prevalent malignancy, largely driven by persistent infection with oncogenic human papillomavirus (HPV). Definitive chemoradiotherapy (CRT) remains the standard of care for locoregional disease; however, a clinically relevant proportion of patients experience persistent or recurrent disease, while outcomes in the metastatic setting remain suboptimal. Recent advances in immune checkpoint inhibition and chemo-immunotherapy combinations are reshaping the therapeutic landscape, raising new questions regarding optimal integration with established treatments and patient selection.
trial_name: Phase 3 study of retifanlimab with carboplatin-paclitaxel in patients with inoperable locally recurrent or metastatic SCAC
